ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic kebab food product class; no specific eggplant-chicken kebab entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for eggplant-chicken kebab as a composite dish. A0CKM (grilled chicken meat) used as the primary protein anchor; eggplant component is not separately coded in FoodEx2 at this composite level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for eggplant chicken kebab as a prepared dish. SNOMED CT coverage for composite grilled dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one skewer of approximately 120 g, comprising alternating pieces of minced chicken and eggplant chunks grilled over charcoal.

nutrient_source.title: Eggplant Kebab Chicken — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Eggplant Kebab Chicken (cr_id G00010367) with confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat (6.5 g/100g) is referenced from the input health_suitability caution note and is consistent with the fat profile of a grilled chicken dish with skin and seasoning.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for eggplant chicken kebab as a composite dish. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on the dominant carbohydrate contributors: eggplant (GI ~15, University of Sydney GI Database) and minor seasoning carbohydrates. The dish is predominantly protein and fat with only 5.24 g carbohydrate per 100 g, yielding a very low glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 6.29) / 100 ≈ 2.2, rounded to 2.5 to account for seasoning variability. Analogous estimate basis: eggplant GI from University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg per 100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, request a reduced-salt preparation and avoid all additional salty condiments, as a single 120 g serving delivers approximately 1296 mg sodium — more than half the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g) and low GI (~35) support glycaemic stability, but high total fat (26.4 g/100 g) may impair post-meal insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content and low glycemic index make this dish acceptable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes, but the high fat content (26.4 g/100 g including 6.5 g saturated fat) should be monitored as it may contribute to insulin resistance over time; avoid pairing with high-GI sides such as white rice or flatbread.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (1080 mg/100 g) and elevated saturated fat (6.5 g/100 g) combined with high total fat (26.4 g/100 g) present compounded cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to its high sodium content (1080 mg/100 g) which raises blood pressure, and its saturated fat content (6.5 g/100 g) which contributes to LDL cholesterol elevation; if consumed occasionally, choose a reduced-salt preparation and pair with non-starchy vegetables.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Eggplant is a nightshade vegetable (solanine content) that may trigger joint inflammation in RA-sensitive individuals; high sodium (1080 mg/100 g) worsens tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should exercise caution with this dish as eggplant contains solanine (a nightshade alkaloid) that may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals, and the high sodium content (1080 mg/100 g) can worsen RA-related tissue swelling; monitor personal response and request reduced-salt preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Eggplant Kebab Chicken is a Middle Eastern-style grilled skewer dish served in Singapore, consisting of minced chicken and chunks of eggplant (aubergine) threaded onto a skewer and cooked over charcoal for a distinctive smoky flavour. The dish is classified under meat and alternatives as a chicken-based preparation. The charcoal grilling method imparts a lightly charred exterior while keeping the interior moist. Eggplant contributes a soft, slightly bitter, and smoky character that complements the seasoned minced chicken. Per 100 g, the dish provides 302 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 26.4 g fat (including 6.5 g saturated fat), and only 5.24 g carbohydrate with 2.85 g sugar, making it a low-carbohydrate, high-fat preparation. Sodium is notably high at 1080 mg per 100 g, which is a primary clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 35 (low), driven by the low carbohydrate content and the low-GI profile of eggplant. For Type 2 Diabetes management, the low carbohydrate and low GI are favourable, but the high fat content warrants monitoring. The dish is halal-suitable and gluten-free in its standard preparation. It is commonly served as a lunch or dinner item in Middle Eastern restaurants and food courts in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010367) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical provenance. Saturated fat value (6.5 g/100 g) is internally consistent with the total fat profile and is referenced from the input health_suitability clinical notes. GI is an analogous estimate based on eggplant GI from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is appropriate given the low carbohydrate content of the dish.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source. This is a confirmed zero per the HPB database entry, which is unusual given eggplant's typical fibre content but is accepted as reported from the canonical source. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific HPB composite dish entry are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. 3. The input description references minced lamb, but the food_name, food_subgroup, and HPB classification are chicken-based; the record is treated as a chicken-based dish per the canonical HPB classification. The description inconsistency is noted for follow-up review. 4. Saturated fat value of 6.5 g/100 g is derived from the clinical caution note in the input health_suitability section and is consistent with the fat profile of a grilled chicken dish; it is not directly reported as a separate field in the HPB per_100g block. 5. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on eggplant GI (~15) from the University of Sydney GI Database; the composite dish GI is estimated conservatively higher to account for seasoning and minor carbohydrate contributors. 6. The input alias '83. Eggplant Kebab Chicken' (menu numbering prefix) is preserved in the flat aliases array for retrieval completeness.
